message:
feat: add Agent Mail config integration and deps status

- Add [agent_mail] section to config.toml with enabled, url, token,
  auto_register, and program_name fields
- Support environment variable overrides: AGENT_MAIL_URL, AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN,
  AGENT_MAIL_ENABLED
- Add 30-second caching to IsAvailable() for fast detection
- Show Agent Mail status in ntm deps output (running/not detected)

Closes: ntm-uwa

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-eqs","title":"Add --tag filtering to ntm send and list","description":"Filter operations by agent tags.

## What This Task Does

Add --tag flag to commands that operate on agents:
- ntm send: Send to agents matching tag
- ntm list: Show only sessions with matching agents
- ntm status: Filter panes by tag
- ntm kill: Kill only agents matching tag

## Interface

$ ntm send --tag=frontend 'Update the UI'
# Sends to all agents tagged 'frontend' across all sessions

$ ntm send myproject --tag=backend 'Run migrations'
# Sends to 'backend' agents in myproject only

$ ntm list --tag=frontend
# Shows sessions that have at least one 'frontend' agent

$ ntm status myproject --tag=backend
# Shows only backend-tagged panes

## Matching Logic

Simple OR matching: agent matches if it has ANY of the specified tags.

$ ntm send --tag=frontend,ui 'message'
# Matches agents with 'frontend' OR 'ui' tag

Future: AND matching with special syntax
$ ntm send --tag='frontend+urgent' 'message'
# Matches agents with 'frontend' AND 'urgent'

## Implementation

1. Add --tag flag to send, list, status, kill commands
2. Parse tags from flag (comma-separated)
3. Filter pane list by tag match
4. Include tag info in JSON output

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --tag works on ntm send
- [ ] --tag works on ntm list
- [ ] --tag works on ntm status  
- [ ] --tag works on ntm kill
- [ ] OR matching for multiple tags
- [ ] Tags included in JSON output

 {"id":"ntm-fpb","title":"Implement command history tracking","description":"Track and expose history of commands sent to agents.

## Rationale
For debugging, auditing, and understanding agent behavior, I need to know:
- What was sent to each agent and when?
- What was the response?
- How long did each command take?

This creates an audit trail and enables analysis.

## Storage
```go
type CommandEntry struct {
    ID        uint64
    Session   string
    Pane      string
    Command   string
    SentAt    time.Time
    AckAt     *time.Time       // when agent acked
    OutputLen int              // lines of output produced
    Duration  *time.Duration   // if completed
}
```

## Query Interface
```bash
ntm --robot-history myproject                    # all history
ntm --robot-history myproject --pane=0.1         # specific pane
ntm --robot-history myproject --last=10          # last N entries
ntm --robot-history myproject --since="1h ago"   # time-based
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "entries": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "pane": "0.1",
      "command": "implement feature X",
      "sent_at": "ISO8601",
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "output_lines": 150,
      "duration_sec": 45
    }
  ],
  "total": 25
}
```

## Persistence
Store in SQLite DB per session:
~/.local/share/ntm/history/{session}.db

## Privacy Consideration
Commands may contain sensitive info. Consider:
- Optional encryption
- Configurable retention period
- Explicit --no-history flag","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:58:36.580871-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-gcv","title":"Implement notification system with desktop and webhook support","description":"Create a notification system that can alert users via multiple channels.

## What This Task Does

Build infrastructure to send notifications when events occur:
- Desktop notifications (native OS)
- Webhook POST (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell command execution
- Log file appending

## Notification Events

- agent.error - Agent hit error state
- agent.crashed - Agent process exited
- agent.restarted - Agent was auto-restarted
- agent.idle - Agent waiting for input
- session.created - New session spawned
- session.killed - Session terminated
- health.degraded - Overall health dropped

## Configuration

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.crashed", "agent.restarted"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"
# Payload template (Go template syntax)
template = '''
{"text": "NTM: {{.Event}} - {{.Message}}"}
'''

[notifications.shell]
command = "~/.config/ntm/notify.sh"
pass_json = true

[notifications.log]
path = "~/.local/share/ntm/notifications.jsonl"

## Implementation

Create internal/notify package:

type Event struct {
    Type      string
    Session   string
    Agent     string
    Message   string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(event Event) error
}

Implementations:
- DesktopNotifier: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- WebhookNotifier: HTTP POST with template rendering
- ShellNotifier: exec.Command with JSON stdin
- LogNotifier: Append to JSONL file

## Desktop Notifications

macOS:
osascript -e 'display notification "message" with title "NTM"'

Linux (notify-send):
notify-send "NTM" "message"

Windows/WSL:
powershell.exe -Command "[Windows.UI.Notifications...]"

## ntm notify command

Manual notification for testing/scripting:

$ ntm notify "Build complete" --session myproject
$ ntm notify --event agent.error --message "Rate limit hit"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Desktop notifications work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Webhook POST sends correctly formatted payload
- [ ] Shell command receives event as JSON
- [ ] Events can be filtered in config
- [ ] ntm notify command for manual triggers
- [ ] Errors in notification don't break NTM

 {"id":"ntm-ozcj","title":"EPIC: Deep CASS Integration - Cross-Agent Session Search","description":"Transform NTM from a session manager into an intelligent AI agent orchestration platform by deeply integrating CASS (Coding Agent Session Search). This enables agents to learn from past sessions, avoid duplicate work, surface institutional knowledge, and provide operators with actionable insights on agent activity.

## Vision
CASS indexes 10+ AI agent conversation histories into a unified, sub-50ms searchable index. Combined with NTM's session management:
- Before solving: Query CASS for similar issues → avoid re-solving
- While working: Surface relevant context from past sessions  
- After solving: New session auto-indexed for future reference
- For operators: Dashboard showing agent activity, trends, insights

## Architecture
- internal/cass/ - Go client for CASS robot mode
- ntm cass <subcommand> - CLI commands for search, status, insights
- --robot-cass-* flags - Machine-readable output
- TUI widgets - CASS search in dashboard

## Success Metrics
- Agents can query CASS in <100ms
- 30% reduction in duplicate problem-solving
- Operators see unified agent activity view","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:27.867341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:27.867341-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.1","title":"CASS Infrastructure: Create internal/cass Go client package","description":"Create the foundational Go client package for CASS integration.

## Overview
This package provides a Go interface to CASS robot mode, enabling NTM to query past agent sessions, check health, and retrieve insights.

## Files to Create
- internal/cass/client.go - Main client with exec wrapper
- internal/cass/types.go - Response types (SearchHit, Status, Capabilities, etc.)
- internal/cass/search.go - Search API wrapper
- internal/cass/health.go - Health check integration
- internal/cass/timeline.go - Timeline API wrapper
- internal/cass/client_test.go - Unit tests with mocks

## Key Design Decisions

### Execution Model
Use os/exec to call cass CLI (not HTTP) because:
- CASS is a local tool, not a server
- Robot mode is designed for CLI consumption
- No need for daemon/socket infrastructure

### Error Handling
- CASS exit codes map to Go errors
- Retry on transient errors (timeout, busy)
- Graceful degradation when CASS not installed

### Response Types
Mirror CASS robot mode JSON schemas:
```go
type SearchHit struct {
    SourcePath  string  `json:"source_path"`
    LineNumber  *int    `json:"line_number,omitempty"`
    Agent       string  `json:"agent"`
    Workspace   string  `json:"workspace"`
    Title       string  `json:"title"`
    Score       float64 `json:"score"`
    Snippet     string  `json:"snippet"`
    CreatedAt   *int64  `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
    MatchType   string  `json:"match_type"`
    Content     string  `json:"content,omitempty"`
}

type SearchResponse struct {
    Query        string       `json:"query"`
    Limit        int          `json:"limit"`
    Offset       int          `json:"offset"`
    Count        int          `json:"count"`
    TotalMatches int          `json:"total_matches"`
    Hits         []SearchHit  `json:"hits"`
    Meta         *Meta        `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
    Aggregations *Aggregations `json:"aggregations,omitempty"`
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client detects if CASS is installed
- [ ] Search returns parsed results
- [ ] Health check works
- [ ] Timeout and retry logic
- [ ] All error codes mapped
- [ ] Tests pass with mock CASS output","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800387-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800387-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800822-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.2","title":"CASS CLI: Add ntm cass subcommand with search, status, insights","description":"Add ntm cass subcommand family for CASS interaction.

## Commands

### ntm cass status
Show CASS index health and statistics.
```
CASS Index Status
  Healthy:        ✓
  Conversations:  1,250
  Messages:       45,000
  Last Indexed:   5 minutes ago
  
Agent Coverage:
  claude_code:    523 sessions
  codex:          312 sessions
  gemini:         189 sessions
  cursor:         156 sessions
  chatgpt:        70 sessions
```

### ntm cass search <query>
Search past sessions with optional filters.
```
ntm cass search "authentication error"
ntm cass search "auth*" --agent claude --since 7d
ntm cass search --workspace /path/to/project
```

Flags:
- --agent <slug>: Filter by agent type
- --workspace <path>: Filter by project
- --since <date>: Time filter (7d, 2w, 2025-01-01)
- --limit <n>: Max results (default 10)
- --json: JSON output

### ntm cass insights
Show AI-powered insights on agent activity.
```
ntm cass insights
ntm cass insights --today
ntm cass insights --week
```

Uses CASS aggregations to show:
- Most active agents
- Common topics
- Error patterns
- Workspace activity

### ntm cass timeline
Show agent activity over time.
```
ntm cass timeline --today --group-by hour
ntm cass timeline --week --group-by day
```

## Implementation
- Add cass.go to internal/cli
- Register as subcommand of root
- Use internal/cass client package
- Support --json for all subcommands

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ntm cass status shows index health
- [ ] ntm cass search returns relevant results
- [ ] ntm cass insights shows aggregated data
- [ ] ntm cass timeline shows activity over time
- [ ] All commands have --json support
- [ ] Help text is comprehensive","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:10.90041-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:10.90041-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:10.900825-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:10.902054-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+{"id":"ntm-ozcj.3","title":"CASS Robot Mode: Add --robot-cass-* flags for AI agent consumption","description":"Add robot mode flags for CASS integration, enabling AI agents to consume CASS data in structured JSON format.

## Flags

### --robot-cass-status
Returns CASS health in JSON format:
```json
{
  "cass_available": true,
  "healthy": true,
  "index": {
    "exists": true,
    "fresh": true,
    "last_indexed_at": 1702569600000,
    "conversations": 1250,
    "messages": 45000
  },
  "agents": {
    "claude_code": 523,
    "codex": 312,
    "gemini": 189
  }
}
```

### --robot-cass-search <query>
Returns search results in JSON:
```json
{
  "query": "authentication error",
  "count": 5,
  "total_matches": 142,
  "hits": [
    {
      "source_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
      "agent": "claude_code",
      "title": "Debug auth flow",
      "score": 8.75,
      "snippet": "...authentication error in OAuth...",
      "created_at": 1702569600000
    }
  ]
}
```

Additional flags:
- --cass-agent <slug>: Filter by agent
- --cass-workspace <path>: Filter by workspace
- --cass-since <date>: Time filter
- --cass-limit <n>: Max results

### --robot-cass-insights
Returns aggregated insights:
```json
{
  "period": "today",
  "agents": {
    "buckets": [
      {"key": "claude_code", "count": 45},
      {"key": "codex", "count": 23}
    ]
  },
  "topics": [
    {"term": "authentication", "count": 12},
    {"term": "database", "count": 8}
  ],
  "errors": [
    {"type": "rate_limit", "count": 3}
  ]
}
```

### --robot-cass-context <query>
Returns relevant past context for spawning agents:
```json
{
  "query": "implement user auth",
  "relevant_sessions": [
    {
      "summary": "Previous auth implementation in this project",
      "key_points": ["Used OAuth2", "JWT tokens", "Redis sessions"],
      "source": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
      "agent": "claude_code",
      "when": "3 days ago"
    }
  ],
  "suggested_context": "When implementing auth, consider: OAuth2 flow from session X, JWT patterns from session Y"
}
```

## Implementation
- Add flags to root.go
- Add handlers in robot.go
- Use internal/cass client
- Error handling with proper exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All flags produce valid JSON
- [ ] Errors are structured JSON
- [ ] Graceful degradation if CASS unavailable
- [ ] Response includes _meta block
- [ ] Works with other robot flags","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:35.028307-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:35.028307-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:35.0289-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:35.030016-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-ozcj.4","title":"CASS TUI Integration: Search widget and activity timeline in dashboard","description":"Integrate CASS into the NTM TUI dashboard for interactive exploration of past sessions.

## Widgets

### CASS Search Palette
Add keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+S) to open CASS search palette:
- Live search-as-you-type
- Results show agent icon, title, timestamp
- Enter to view details in pane
- Tab to filter by agent

### Activity Sparkline
Add agent activity sparkline to status bar:
```
📊 [▁▂▃▅▆▇█▆▄▂▁] 24h activity | 45 sessions today
```

### Past Conversation Browser
Add panel (toggle with F9) showing:
- Recent sessions across all agents
- Filter by agent, workspace
- Click to expand conversation
- Link to open in CASS TUI

### CASS Status Indicator
In status bar, show CASS health:
- 🔍 CASS: ✓ (healthy, fresh index)
- 🔍 CASS: ⚠ (stale index)
- 🔍 CASS: ✗ (not available)

## Implementation
- Add cass widget to internal/tui/components
- Integrate with existing dashboard model
- Use async loading to avoid blocking
- Cache results for performance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search palette opens with Ctrl+S
- [ ] Activity sparkline updates live
- [ ] Conversation browser shows recent sessions
- [ ] Status indicator in status bar
- [ ] Smooth transitions, no flicker
- [ ] Works without CASS (graceful degradation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:54.05798-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:54.05798-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:54.058486-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:54.060262-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:30:54.061408-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-ozcj.5","title":"CASS Context Injection: Provide relevant past context when spawning agents","description":"Automatically inject relevant past context when spawning new agents, reducing duplicate problem-solving.

## Overview
When `ntm spawn` or `ntm add` creates a new agent pane, query CASS for relevant past sessions based on:
- Current project/workspace
- Prompt content (if provided)
- Recent activity in the session

## Implementation

### Spawn with Context
```bash
# Auto-context mode (default when CASS available)
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --prompt="implement OAuth"
# CASS query: workspace:/path/to/myproject AND "OAuth"
# Injects: "Previous OAuth work in this project: [summary]"

# Explicit context query
ntm spawn myproject --cc=1 --cass-context="authentication patterns"
# Injects relevant past sessions about auth

# Disable context
ntm spawn myproject --cc=1 --no-cass-context
```

### Context Injection Format
```
## Relevant Past Sessions

This project has prior work on similar topics:

1. **OAuth Implementation** (3 days ago, claude_code)
   - Used passport.js with OAuth2 strategy
   - JWT tokens stored in Redis
   - Key file: src/auth/oauth.ts
   
2. **Session Management** (1 week ago, codex)
   - Express sessions with Redis store
   - CORS configuration for cookies
   
Consider reviewing these before re-implementing.
```

### Flags
- --cass-context <query>: Explicit context query
- --no-cass-context: Disable auto-context
- --cass-context-limit <n>: Max sessions to include (default 3)
- --cass-context-days <n>: Look back N days (default 30)

### Robot Mode
```bash
ntm --robot-spawn-context myproject --prompt="implement OAuth"
# Returns JSON with suggested context
{
  "workspace": "/path/to/myproject",
  "prompt": "implement OAuth",
  "relevant_sessions": [...],
  "suggested_injection": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn queries CASS for relevant context
- [ ] Context injected into agent prompt
- [ ] Can disable with --no-cass-context
- [ ] Works with spawn, add, send commands
- [ ] Graceful when CASS unavailable
- [ ] Robot mode returns context JSON","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.45423-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.45423-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.455375-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.457942-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.458831-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-ozcj.6","title":"CASS Duplicate Detection: Warn before solving already-solved problems","description":"Detect and warn when an agent is about to work on something that was already solved in a past session.

## Overview
When sending prompts to agents, check CASS for similar past work and optionally:
- Show warning to user
- Inject "check this first" context to agent
- Link to past session for reference

## Implementation

### Send with Duplicate Check
```bash
ntm send myproject --all "fix the authentication bug"
# CASS check: "fix authentication bug" in recent sessions
# If found:
⚠ Similar work found in past sessions:
  1. "Debug auth flow" (2 days ago, claude_code)
     Preview: Fixed JWT validation issue in middleware
     
  Continue anyway? [y/N/view]
  - y: Send as-is
  - N: Cancel
  - view: Open past session in 
```

### Flags
- --cass-check: Force duplicate check (default: on)
- --no-cass-check: Skip duplicate check
- --cass-similarity <n>: Similarity threshold (0-100, default 70)
- --cass-check-days <n>: Look back N days (default 7)

### Robot Mode
```bash
ntm --robot-check-duplicates myproject --msg="fix auth bug"
{
  "query": "fix auth bug",
  "duplicates_found": true,
  "similar_sessions": [
    {
      "source_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
      "title": "Debug auth flow",
      "similarity": 0.85,
      "agent": "claude_code",
      "when": "2 days ago",
      "preview": "Fixed JWT validation..."
    }
  ],
  "recommendation": "review_before_proceeding"
}
```

### Integration Points
- ntm send: Check before broadcasting
- ntm prompt (if added): Check before sending
- TUI: Show warning in palette before confirming send

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Duplicate check on send command
- [ ] User can view, skip, or proceed
- [ ] Configurable similarity threshold
- [ ] Robot mode returns check results
- [ ] Works in TUI palette

 {"id":"ntm-pb3","title":"Implement --robot-interrupt for priority course correction","description":"Interrupt an agent with high-priority instruction.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need to redirect an agent urgently - stop what you're doing, do this instead. Regular send just queues; interrupt needs to actually break the agent's current flow.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-interrupt myproject --pane=0.1 --msg="STOP: critical bug found, fix X first"
ntm --robot-interrupt myproject --all --msg="Halt all work, sync for team discussion"
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "interrupted": ["0.1"],
  "method": "ctrl_c_then_send",
  "previous_states": {
    "0.1": {"state": "active", "last_output": "..."}
  },
  "message_sent": true,
  "ready_for_input": true
}
```

## Interrupt Methods
1. Send Ctrl+C to break current operation
2. Wait for prompt/ready state
3. Send the interrupt message

## Options
- --force: Send Ctrl+C even if agent appears idle
- --no-wait: Don't wait for ready state
- --preserve-context: Try to save agent's context before interrupting

## Safety Considerations
- Log interrupted state for potential resume
- Avoid interrupting mid-file-write
- Rate limit to prevent interrupt storms","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:24.290441-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:24.290441-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-plr","title":"Implement custom agent definitions via plugin files","description":"Allow users to define custom agent types in config files.

## What This Task Does

Enable users to add new agent types without modifying NTM code.
Agent definitions are TOML files in ~/.config/ntm/agents/.

## Plugin Location

~/.config/ntm/agents/
├── cursor.toml
├── aider.toml
└── local-llm.toml

## File Format

# ~/.config/ntm/agents/cursor.toml
[agent]
name = "cursor"           # Used in --cursor=N flag
alias = "cur"             # Short form
command = "cursor --headless --ai-mode"
description = "Cursor AI editor in headless mode"

[agent.env]
CURSOR_HEADLESS = "1"

[agent.defaults]
tags = ["cursor", "editor"]

## Loading Plugins

On startup:
1. Scan ~/.config/ntm/agents/*.toml
2. Parse each file into AgentPlugin struct
3. Register with spawn/add commands
4. Generate aliases for shell integration

type AgentPlugin struct {
    Name        string
    Alias       string
    Command     string
    Description string
    Env         map[string]string
    DefaultTags []string
}

## Integration with spawn

$ ntm spawn myproject --cursor=2
# Spawns 2 panes running: cursor --headless --ai-mode

$ ntm spawn myproject --cur=1
# Same, using alias

## Shell Integration

'ntm init zsh' generates aliases for plugins:
alias cur='cursor --headless --ai-mode'

## Discovery

$ ntm plugins list
┌─────────┬───────┬─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Name    │ Alias │ Description                     │
├─────────┬───────┬─────────────────────────────────┤
│ cursor  │ cur   │ Cursor AI editor in headless... │
│ aider   │ aid   │ Aider coding assistant          │
│ local   │ loc   │ Local Ollama LLM                │
└─────────┴───────┴─────────────────────────────────┘

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent TOML files parsed correctly
- [ ] Custom agents work with spawn
- [ ] Custom agents work with add
- [ ] Aliases generated in shell init
- [ ] ntm plugins list shows custom agents
- [ ] Environment variables applied

-{"id":"ntm-uwa","title":"Implement Agent Mail server detection and config","description":"# Implement Agent Mail Server Detection and Config

## Purpose

Before using any Agent Mail features, NTM needs to detect if the server is running and load configuration.

## Configuration Schema

Add to ~/.config/ntm/config.toml:
```toml
[agent_mail]
enabled = true                              # Master toggle
url = "http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/"          # Server endpoint
token = ""                                  # Bearer token (or use env var)
auto_register = true                        # Auto-register sessions as agents
program_name = "ntm"                        # Program identifier for registration
```

Environment variable overrides:
- AGENT_MAIL_URL
- AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN
- AGENT_MAIL_ENABLED (0/1)

## Detection Logic

```go
func (c *Client) IsAvailable() bool {
    // 1. Check if enabled in config
    // 2. Attempt health_check() with 2s timeout
    // 3. Cache result for 30s to avoid repeated checks
}

func (c *Client) RequireAvailable() error {
    if !c.IsAvailable() {
        return ErrAgentMailUnavailable
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Integration Points

1. **ntm deps**: Show Agent Mail status
   ```
   Agent Mail: ✓ running (http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/)
   Agent Mail: ✗ not detected (optional)
   ```

2. **Config loading**: internal/config loads agent_mail section
3. **Lazy initialization**: Client created on first use, not at startup
4. **Status command**: Show connection status in ntm status

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config schema added with sensible defaults
- [ ] Environment variables override config
- [ ] Detection is fast (<2s timeout) and cached
- [ ] ntm deps shows Agent Mail status
- [ ] Graceful behavior when server unavailable","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:01.67162-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:49.438391-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-uwa","depends_on_id":"ntm-ceb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.651713-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-y44","title":"EPIC: Advanced Robot Orchestration Flags","description":"Comprehensive robot mode flags for AI orchestrator control. Enables unified state queries, delta updates, batch operations, and cross-tool correlation. Priority based on orchestration value.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:49:44.994993-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:49:44.994993-05:00","comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Orchestrator's Perspective: Why These Flags Matter

This epic represents a fundamental shift in how NTM serves AI orchestrators. The existing --robot-status flag is just the beginning. Here's the complete vision:

### Core Philosophy
An AI orchestrator managing 10+ agents across multiple sessions needs:
1. **Complete situational awareness** in a single query
2. **Token efficiency** to maximize useful work vs overhead
3. **Fast feedback loops** for responsive orchestration
4. **Cross-tool correlation** (tmux ↔ beads ↔ agent-mail)

### The Token Economics
Consider orchestrating 8 agents:
- Current approach: 8+ tool calls, ~2000 tokens of output to parse
- With --robot-snapshot: 1 call, ~500 tokens, everything I need
- With --since delta: 1 call, ~50 tokens for incremental updates

This is a 40x improvement in polling scenarios.

### Dependency Structure
The implementation follows a logical build-up:
1. **Foundation**: Agent detection (ntm-3n9) + Alert system (ntm-rlp)
2. **Integrations**: Agent-mail (ntm-gk5) + Beads (ntm-l5y)
3. **Core**: Unified snapshot (ntm-sim)
4. **Extensions**: Delta mode (ntm-dvz), Terse (ntm-oba), Health (ntm-c36)
5. **Advanced**: Graph (ntm-i5x), Assign (ntm-20n), Interrupt (ntm-pb3)

### Success Criteria
- Single --robot-snapshot call provides complete state
- Delta queries return only changes (95% token savings)
- All output is structured JSON, no parsing human text
- Cross-tool correlation works (know which agent → which bead → which thread)
- Batch operations support multi-agent commands","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:55:21Z"},{"id":13,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Revised Architecture: Minimal Dependencies

After review, the dependency structure has been revised. Core robot flags should work IMMEDIATELY without requiring integrations. Integrations ENHANCE but don't BLOCK.

### Tier 1: Core (No blockers - implement first)
- **ntm-sim**: --robot-snapshot - unified state query
- **ntm-ign**: --robot-tail - multi-pane output
- **ntm-d2t**: --robot-send - batch messaging  
- **ntm-c36**: --robot-health - health matrix
- **ntm-oln**: --robot-spawn - structured session creation
- **ntm-pb3**: --robot-interrupt - priority course correction

### Tier 2: Extensions (depend on Tier 1)
- **ntm-dvz**: --robot-snapshot --since - delta updates (needs ntm-sim + ntm-hat)
- **ntm-oba**: --robot-terse - compact output (needs ntm-sim)
- **ntm-a8q**: --robot-ack - send confirmation

### Tier 3: Enhancements (enhance Tier 1 but don't block)
- **ntm-3n9**: Agent detection (improves accuracy of all queries)
- **ntm-rlp**: Alert generation (adds alerts to snapshot/health)
- **ntm-gk5**: Agent-mail integration (adds mail data to snapshot)
- **ntm-l5y**: Beads integration (adds bead data to snapshot)
- **ntm-hat**: State tracking ring buffer (enables delta mode)

### Tier 4: Advanced Features
- **ntm-20n**: --robot-assign - work distribution recommendations
- **ntm-i5x**: --robot-graph - cross-tool correlation
- **ntm-sc6**: Progress detection
- **ntm-fpb**: Command history tracking
- **ntm-lla**: Session save/restore

### Implementation Order Recommendation
1. Core Tier 1 flags (can be implemented in parallel)
2. State tracking (ntm-hat) for delta support
3. Delta mode (ntm-dvz) 
4. Enhancements as needed
5. Advanced features based on user demand","created_at":"2025-12-10T07:59:37Z"},{"id":14,"issue_id":"ntm-y44","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Child Tasks

This epic tracks the following tasks:

### Core Robot Flags (Tier 1 - No blockers)
- ntm-sim: --robot-snapshot (unified state query)
- ntm-ign: --robot-tail (multi-pane output)
- ntm-d2t: --robot-send (batch messaging)
- ntm-c36: --robot-health (health matrix)
- ntm-oln: --robot-spawn (structured session creation)
- ntm-pb3: --robot-interrupt (priority course correction)

### Extensions (Tier 2)
- ntm-dvz: --robot-snapshot --since (delta updates)
- ntm-oba: --robot-terse (ultra-compact output)
- ntm-a8q: --robot-ack (send confirmation)

### Enhancements (Tier 3)
- ntm-3n9: Agent type detection
- ntm-rlp: Alert generation and tracking
- ntm-gk5: Agent-mail integration
- ntm-l5y: Beads integration
- ntm-hat: State change ring buffer

### Advanced Features (Tier 4)
- ntm-20n: --robot-assign (work distribution)
- ntm-i5x: --robot-graph (correlation map)
- ntm-sc6: Progress detection
- ntm-fpb: Command history tracking
- ntm-lla: Session save/restore

Total: 19 tasks across 4 tiers","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:00:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-y7l","title":"Implement agent tagging in pane titles","description":"Store and parse agent tags in tmux pane titles.

## What This Task Does

Extend the pane title format to include user-defined tags:
- Current: session__cc_1
- New: session__cc_1[frontend,refactor]

## Tag Storage Format

Tags are comma-separated within square brackets at the end of pane title.

Examples:
- myproject__cc_1[frontend]
- myproject__cod_1[backend,api,urgent]
- myproject__gmi_1[] (no tags, explicit empty)

## Implementation

Add to internal/tmux package:

func SetPaneTags(session string, paneIndex int, tags []string) error {
    currentTitle := GetPaneTitle(session, paneIndex)
    baseTitle := stripTags(currentTitle)
    newTitle := baseTitle + "[" + strings.Join(tags, ",") + "]"
    return exec.Command("tmux", "select-pane", "-t", target, "-T", newTitle).Run()
}

func GetPaneTags(session string, paneIndex int) []string {
    title := GetPaneTitle(session, paneIndex)
    // Parse [tag1,tag2] from end of title
    ...
}

## Integration with spawn/add

$ ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --tags=frontend
# Creates: myproject__cc_1[frontend], myproject__cc_2[frontend]

$ ntm add myproject --cod=1 --tags=backend,api
# Creates: myproject__cod_1[backend,api]

## Tag modification

$ ntm tag myproject:1 --add=urgent
$ ntm tag myproject:1 --remove=frontend
$ ntm tag myproject:1 --set=backend,api

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tags stored in pane title
- [ ] Tags parsed correctly from title
- [ ] --tags flag works on spawn command
- [ ] --tags flag works on add command
- [ ] ntm tag command for modification
- [ ] Tags survive session detach/reattach

-{"id":"ntm-yxa","title":"Implement session agent registration","description":"# Implement Session Agent Registration

## Purpose

Each NTM session should register as an agent with Agent Mail, enabling it to send/receive messages and reserve files on behalf of the session.

## Registration Flow

When a session is created or attached:
```go
func RegisterSessionAgent(client *agentmail.Client, sessionName string) (*agentmail.Agent, error) {
    // 1. Ensure project exists (uses session's working directory)
    projectKey := getSessionWorkingDir(sessionName)
    client.EnsureProject(projectKey)
    
    // 2. Register agent with deterministic name
    agentName := fmt.Sprintf("ntm_%s", sanitize(sessionName))
    return client.RegisterAgent(RegisterAgentInput{
        ProjectKey:      projectKey,
        Program:         "ntm",
        Model:           "coordinator",
        Name:            agentName,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf("NTM session coordinator for %s", sessionName),
    })
}
```

## Naming Convention

- Agent name: `ntm_{session_name}` (e.g., `ntm_myproject`)
- Sanitization: Replace non-alphanumeric with underscore, lowercase
- If name taken: Append timestamp suffix

## Integration Points

1. **ntm spawn**: Register agent after session creation
2. **ntm attach**: Update agent's last_active_ts
3. **ntm kill**: Optionally notify other agents before shutdown
4. **Session metadata**: Store agent_name in session context

## Storing Agent Identity

Track registered agent in session state:
```go
type SessionAgentInfo struct {
    AgentName    string    `json:"agent_name"`
    ProjectKey   string    `json:"project_key"`
    RegisteredAt time.Time `json:"registered_at"`
}
```

Store in: ~/.config/ntm/sessions/{session_name}/agent.json

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sessions auto-register as agents on spawn
- [ ] Agent identity persisted for session lifetime
- [ ] Attach updates last_active_ts
- [ ] Registration failures don't block session creation
- [ ] Agent name derivation is deterministic and unique","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:03.476453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:03.476453-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yxa","depends_on_id":"ntm-uwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.757544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Purpose

Each NTM session should register as an agent with Agent Mail, enabling it to send/receive messages and reserve files on behalf of the session.

## Registration Flow

When a session is created or attached:
```go
func RegisterSessionAgent(client *agentmail.Client, sessionName string) (*agentmail.Agent, error) {
    // 1. Ensure project exists (uses session's working directory)
    projectKey := getSessionWorkingDir(sessionName)
    client.EnsureProject(projectKey)
    
    // 2. Register agent with deterministic name
    agentName := fmt.Sprintf("ntm_%s", sanitize(sessionName))
    return client.RegisterAgent(RegisterAgentInput{
        ProjectKey:      projectKey,
        Program:         "ntm",
        Model:           "coordinator",
        Name:            agentName,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf("NTM session coordinator for %s", sessionName),
    })
}
```

## Naming Convention

- Agent name: `ntm_{session_name}` (e.g., `ntm_myproject`)
- Sanitization: Replace non-alphanumeric with underscore, lowercase
- If name taken: Append timestamp suffix

## Integration Points

1. **ntm spawn**: Register agent after session creation
2. **ntm attach**: Update agent's last_active_ts
3. **ntm kill**: Optionally notify other agents before shutdown
4. **Session metadata**: Store agent_name in session context

## Storing Agent Identity

Track registered agent in session state:
```go
type SessionAgentInfo struct {
    AgentName    string    `json:"agent_name"`
    ProjectKey   string    `json:"project_key"`
    RegisteredAt time.Time `json:"registered_at"`
}
```

Store in: ~/.config/ntm/sessions/{session_name}/agent.json

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sessions auto-register as agents on spawn
- [ ] Agent identity persisted for session lifetime
- [ ] Attach updates last_active_ts
- [ ] Registration failures don't block session creation
- [ ] Agent name derivation is deterministic and unique","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:03.476453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:53.730223-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yxa","depends_on_id":"ntm-uwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.757544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-z2k","title":"Update pane naming to include model identifier","description":"# Update Pane Naming to Include Model Identifier

## Purpose

Enhance pane titles to include model information for identification and filtering.

## Current Naming

`{session}__{type}_{index}`
Example: `myproject__cc_1`

## New Naming

`{session}__{type}_{model}_{index}`
Example: `myproject__cc_opus_1`

Or alternatively with underscore separator:
`{session}__{type}_{index}_{model}`
Example: `myproject__cc_1_opus`

## Considerations

1. **Backwards compatibility**: Old sessions still work
2. **Model normalization**: Convert to safe identifier (lowercase, no special chars)
3. **Parsing**: Need to extract model from pane name
4. **Length limits**: tmux pane titles have practical limits

## Recommended Format

Use suffix format for easier parsing:
```
myproject__cc_1          # No model (default)
myproject__cc_1_opus     # With model
myproject__cc_1_sonnet   # With model
```

Regex: `(.+)__(\w+)_(\d+)(?:_(\w+))?`
Groups: session, type, index, model (optional)

## Pane Type Detection Update

```go
func ParsePaneName(title string) (*PaneInfo, error) {
    re := regexp.MustCompile(`(.+)__(\w+)_(\d+)(?:_(\w+))?`)
    matches := re.FindStringSubmatch(title)
    if matches == nil {
        return nil, ErrInvalidPaneName
    }
    return &PaneInfo{
        Session: matches[1],
        Type:    AgentType(matches[2]),
        Index:   parseInt(matches[3]),
        Model:   matches[4], // May be empty
    }, nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New panes include model in title
- [ ] Model normalized to safe identifier
- [ ] ParsePaneName handles both old and new formats
- [ ] status command shows model info
- [ ] dashboard shows model info
- [ ] Filtering by model works (--cc:opus)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:43.467199-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:43.467199-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-z2k","depends_on_id":"ntm-057","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:21:53.966599-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
